sonnet_review.biasedText: President Trump wasted no time asserting his second-term agenda Monday
sonnet_review.reason: The phrase appears in the article and carries a favorable, energetic connotation; a neutral alternative could have been used.
This wording is present and adopts a dramatic, evaluative tone (“dismantling”) rather than neutral terms like “reversing.”
This phrasing is in the text and diminishes the prior administration’s initiatives, reflecting subjective framing.

gpt_review.biasedText: President Trump wasted no time asserting his second-term agenda Monday – signing dozens of executive orders in a flurry of activity that began just moments after taking the oath of office.
gpt_review.reason: These phrases are in the piece and inject urgency and positive spin beyond strictly factual description.

gpt_review.biasedText: Trump, 78, kicked off his avalanche of Day 1 actions at the US Capitol
gpt_review.reason: “Avalanche” is a vivid, dramatic metaphor present in the text that amplifies the sense of scale and momentum.

gpt_review.biasedText: The packed arena erupted in cheers as the president put ink to paper and then tossed the pens into the crowd, allowing a few of his lucky supporters to go home with historic souvenirs.
gpt_review.reason: The language (“erupted,” “lucky,” “historic souvenirs”) is celebratory and subjective, framing the scene in a laudatory way.

gpt_review.biasedText: in a stark departure from Biden, simultaneously answered questions from the press.
gpt_review.reason: “Stark departure” is an evaluative intensifier; a more neutral comparison could have been used.

gpt_review.biasedText: Trump's expected to sign even more executive orders in the coming days to quickly make good on his many campaign promises.
gpt_review.reason: “Quickly make good on his many campaign promises” positively frames intent and effectiveness beyond neutral reporting.
